metadata.title: States PCS: Rajasthan GK Questions for RPSC (RAS) Examinations

questions.question: The temple city of Osean in Rajasthan was established by which of the following Rajputs?
questions.explanation: Gurjar Pratiharas established Marwar in Rajasthan and built the Temple city of Osean, near Jodhpur. Their origin is from Mandsaur and notable Kings are Nagabhatta-I, Nagabhatta-II and Mihirbhoja.

questions.question: Who among the following Kings of Rajasthan was a victim of Patricide?
questions.explanation: Rana Kumbha was killed by his son Udai Singh I in 1468. However, Uday Singh died or murdered in 1473 and was succeeded by his brother Raimal. Raimal was later succeeded by his son Sangram Singh or Rana Sanga. Thus, Rana Sanga was grandson of Rana Kumbha.

questions.question: Tower of Victory or Vijay Stambh in Rajasthan is located in__?
questions.explanation: The Vijaya Stambha is an imposing victory monument located within Chittor Fort in Chittorgarh, Rajasthan dedicated to Vishnu. The tower was constructed by the Mewar king, Rana Kumbha, in 1448. It is to commemorate his victory over the combined armies of Malwa and Gujarat led by Mahmud Khilji.

questions.question: Which among the following is not located in Rajasthan?
questions.explanation: Narwar Fort is located in the Shivpuri district of Madhya Pradesh. The fort is situated on a hilltop, about 500 feet above ground level, and covers an area of 8 square kilometers. The fort is surrounded by the Kali Sindh River, which forms a natural moat. The fort is said to have been built or rebuilt by the Kachwaha Rajputs in the 10th century. It is also known for the love story of Nal and Damayanti.

questions.question: Who among the following established Mewar Kingdom in Rajasthan ?
questions.explanation: Mewar or Mewad is a region in the south-central part of Rajasthan state of India. It was established by Bappa Rawal. The bardic chronicles describe him as a member of the Guhila clan of Rajputs, and some of these consider him as the founder of the Guhila dynasty.

questions.question: Rajasthani and Pahari schools of art forms were famous for which of the following art forms?
questions.explanation: The new patrons of paintings came up with Rajasthan and Pahari kingdom schools. These were the schools that illustrated manuscripts, album miniatures, portraits, and various other paintings made their way all over the India.

questions.question: Which of the following annual fairs of Rajasthan is famous for its camel trading event?
questions.explanation: The Pushkar Fair, also called the Pushkar Camel Fair or locally as Kartik Mela or Pushkar ka Mela is an annual multi-day livestock fair and cultural fete held in the town of Pushkar. It is famous for its camel trading event.

questions.question: Soils of Western Rajasthan have a high content of which of the following minerals? (UPSC Prelims 1993)
questions.explanation: Soil of Western Rajasthan have a high content of Calcium. These soils have rich content of iron- oxide and devoid of calcium salts because calcium salts soluble in water and are easily washed away.

questions.question: Which disease, primarily affecting mine workers in Rajasthan, is specifically targeted by the state government policy?
questions.explanation: Silicosis is a lung disease caused by inhaling fine silica dust, common in mining and construction. The Rajasthan government has implemented policies to support affected workers, recognizing silicosis as a significant occupational hazard. Under the Persons with Disabilities Act, 1995, those diagnosed with silicosis may receive disability benefits, highlighting the importance of workplace safety and health regulations in high-risk industries.

questions.question: The Chief Minister is Excluded within the jurisdiction of Lokayukta in which among the following states?1. Odisha2. Uttar Pradesh3. Rajasthan4. MaharashtraChoose the correct option from the codes given below :
questions.explanation: The Chief Minister is included within the jurisdiction of Lokayukta in- Himachal Pradesh, Andhra Pradesh, Madhya Pradesh, Gujarat. And the excluded states are Odisha, Maharashtra, Uttar Pradesh, Rajasthan, Bihar.
